images.caption:
Figure 1:Claw-Eval architecture. The framework operates across three temporal phases (Setup, Execution, Judge) and three spatial layers (Host Machine, Isolation Environment, Mock Services). During execution, the agent works within the isolation environment, interacting with workspace files, mock services, and system-layer tools. A temporal firewall separates execution from grading: the grader consumes three independent evidence channels
(execution traces, environment snapshots, and server-side audit logs) that are never exposed to the agent during execution.
Figure 2:Pass^3 rate by difficulty level. All models degrade from Easy to Hard.
Figure 2:Pass^3 rate by difficulty level. All models degrade from Easy to Hard.
(a)Safety violations: per-model count of tasks where the hybrid pipeline detects a violation, split into jointly detected and missed by the vanilla judge.
(a)Safety violations: per-model count of tasks where the hybrid pipeline detects a violation, split into jointly detected and missed by the vanilla judge.
(b)Robustness issues: per-model count of tasks where the hybrid pipeline detects robustness issues, split into jointly detected and missed by the vanilla judge.
(a)Pass@3 (solid) and Pass^3 (dashed) as the error injection rate increases from 0.0 to 0.6.
(a)Pass@3 (solid) and Pass^3 (dashed) as the error injection rate increases from 0.0 to 0.6.
(b)The Pass@3 – Pass^3 gap widens monotonically for every model, quantifying growing inconsistency.
(a)Average rounds vs. Pass^3. Near-zero correlation is shown (r=0.07r=0.07,R2<0.01R^{2}<0.01). Darker shading indicates more desirable regions.
(a)Average rounds vs. Pass^3. Near-zero correlation is shown (r=0.07r=0.07,R2<0.01R^{2}<0.01). Darker shading indicates more desirable regions.
(b)Question precision vs. Pass^3. Question precision explains 76% of Pass^3 variance (r=0.87r=0.87,R2=0.76R^{2}=0.76). Shaded region shows the 95% confidence band.
Table 6:Pass^3 (%) by model and multimodal domain. Best per column inbold; second bestunderlined. Each domain has a different leader.
Figure 6:Pass@3 (dashed) vs. Pass^3 (solid) across multimodal domains. The ratiorrindicates the consistency conversion rate.
